channel.title: The Sentinel Review — National Security Feed
channel.description: Automated national security news aggregation. Updated every 8 hours.

item.title: 📍 MQ-9 Reaper Slings Drone-Killing Laser-Guided Rockets In Tests | The War Zone
item.description: The MQ-9 successfully tested APKWS rockets against air and ground targets, as the demand for lower-cost counter-drone weapons surges. The post MQ-9 Reaper Slings Drone-Killing Laser-Guided Rockets In Tests appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 🔐 NDS 2026 – More resourcing for a complex cyber threat environment | ASPI Strategist
item.description: Australia’s 2026 National Defence Strategy (NDS) and Integrated Investment Program (IIP) demonstrate a stronger commitment to cyber defence and capability. They reinforce that cyber for Defence is not just about securing networks but also about ...

item.title: 🌏 Five outcomes that would make Trump’s trip to China a success | Atlantic Council
item.description: Though surprises are always possible, the meetings in Beijing will likely be defined by a narrow set of deliverables. The post Five outcomes that would make Trump’s trip to China a success appeared first on Atlantic Council.

item.title: 🌍 Possibility Of Operation To Retrieve Iran’s Enriched Uranium Appears To Rise As Negotiations Sputter | The War Zone
item.description: Both Trump and Netanyahu made comments in the last 24 hours that point to a possible high-risk operation to snatch Iran's nuclear material. The post Possibility Of Operation To Retrieve Iran’s Enriched Uranium Appears To Rise As Negotiations Sputter appeared first on The War…

item.title: 🏴 Saab launches new Carl-Gustaf, air defense munitions, expands production in US, India | Breaking Defense
item.description: The Swedish defense firm has revealed new warheads and provided details on delivery timelines from its new production sites outside Europe.

item.title: 📍 Community trust powers energy growth in the United States | Atlantic Council
item.description: To power rapid economic and technological changes in the US, utilities must strengthen systems with speed, discipline, and trust. The post Community trust powers energy growth in the United States appeared first on Atlantic Council.

item.title: 🏴 Anti-Drone “Cope Cage” Appears On Russian Patrol Boat | The War Zone
item.description: Ukrainian drone attacks on naval targets are driving the development of new defenses, but overhead screens alone have dwindling efficacy. The post Anti-Drone “Cope Cage” Appears On Russian Patrol Boat appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 🏛️ Navy unveils three new PAEs for aviation, mission systems, munitions | Breaking Defense
item.description: “The establishment of these PAEs today will accelerate acquisition efforts in three key portfolios,” said Jason Potter, who is performing the duties of assistant secretary of the Navy for research and development.

item.title: 🏴 Sensationalism Doesn’t Serve Society | War on the Rocks
item.description: Welcome to The Ukraine Compass, a weekly digest of Ukrainian commentary and analysis from across the political spectrum only for War on the Rocks members. Each Monday, we bring you a curated selection of articles from Ukrainian media offering insight into how Ukrainians…

item.title: 🌍 Kroenig published in Foreign Policy on Iran’s uranium enrichment | Atlantic Council
item.description: On May 11, Atlantic Council vice president and Scowcroft Center senior director Matthew Kroenig published an article in Foreign Policy, arguing that Iran does not have a right to enrich uranium and that Trump should push for zero enrichment in perpetuity as part of any deal with…

item.title: 🛡️ 5 US bases selected for anti-drone pilot program | Defense News
item.description: Two military installations along the southern border are among the five that will receive anti-drone energy systems.

item.title: 🕵️ Turkey’s STM debuts new unmanned systems, is ‘really open’ to Gulf collaboration | Breaking Defense
item.description: “We are already using artificial intelligence, image processing, machine learning and things like that. But we are also going more deep and deep in those areas,” the company’s general manager told Breaking Defense.

item.title: 🌏 Trump Goes to Beijing: What to Watch | The Diplomat
item.description: China’s belated confirmation of the visit reflects Beijing’s broader approach to Washington under Trump’s second administration: do not initiate, do not refuse, and do not compromise.

item.title: 📍 C-5 Galaxies Now Slated To Keep Flying Until 2050 As Readiness Plummets To 37 Percent | The War Zone
item.description: Nothing in the USAF's inventory can do what a C-5 can, but even after costly upgrades, they remain extremely temperamental. The post C-5 Galaxies Now Slated To Keep Flying Until 2050 As Readiness Plummets To 37 Percent appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 🌏 Taiwan’s parliament passes pared-back supplementary defense budget | Breaking Defense
item.description: The approved supplemental budget is much lower than the NT$1.25 trillion proposed by the Lai administration, with the funding going towards two separate US arms packages for Taiwan.

item.title: 📍 Litigation Tracker: Legal Challenges to Trump Administration Actions | Just Security
item.description: A public resource tracking all the legal challenges to the Trump administration's executive orders and actions. The post Litigation Tracker: Legal Challenges to Trump Administration Actions appeared first on Just Security.

item.title: 🌍 Iran War Chokes ‘Major Driver’ of Global Economy | Foreign Policy
item.description: The Hormuz crisis is hitting diesel even harder than crude.

item.title: 🏴 Ukraine and Russia fight on despite US-mediated ceasefire | Defense News
item.description: Russia and Ukraine reported fighting along their front line despite agreeing to a ceasefire from May 9 to May 11 as part of a Trump-led push for peace.

item.title: 🌏 Trump Must Put Detained Uyghur Intellectuals on the Agenda for Xi Summit | The Diplomat
item.description: If Trump sits across from Xi and fails to raise the cases of Uyghur Americans and their families, Beijing will read that omission not as pragmatism, but as weakness.

item.title: 🕵️ Canada eyes Turkey as a fellow mid-power ally in weapons development | Defense News
item.description: Canada looks for lessons in Turkey's effort to nurse a homegrown industrial ecosystem for making vital defense equipment, a senior Canadian official said.

item.title: 🏴 Europe Shrugs Off Trump’s Latest Threats | Foreign Policy
item.description: Facing U.S. troop withdrawal, the continent’s leaders feel less alarmed and better prepared.

item.title: 🕵️ Unearthing a Colombian Politician’s Connections to Neo-Nazi Active Club Group | Bellingcat
item.description: This investigation is a collaboration between Bellingcat and Colombian media outlet Cerosetenta. You can read Cerosetenta’s piece in Spanish here. A video posted on Feb. 26 shows several men painting over graffiti in Restrepo, a neighbourhood in Bogota, Colombia, and replacing…

item.title: 🏛️ Epic Fury has Navy rethinking carrier deployment tempo | Defense News
item.description: The record-breaking deployment of the USS Gerald R. Ford has reopened the debate about how the Navy should structure deployments and time at home.

item.title: 🌍 Who Wants Hezbollah to Stay Armed? | Foreign Policy
item.description: Grievances with the Lebanese state, not sectarian loyalty, are driving popular support for the group retaining its guns.

item.title: 🌍 Allies at War: Japan-US Relations From the War on Terror to Today’s Iran Conflict | The Diplomat
item.description: Insights from Tokyo's handling of the Iraq War could help Japan navigate the current demands from the United States regarding the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: 🌏 China’s Malacca Dilemma, After Hormuz | Foreign Policy
item.description: Western-dominated insurance premiums can choke off Beijing’s oil supplies more effectively than warships can.

item.title: 🏛️ Remains of missing soldier found off the coast of Morocco | Defense News
item.description: The remains of a U.S. soldier reported missing in Southern Morocco last week have been recovered, the Army said in a statement Sunday.

item.title: 🏴 Kazakh and Uzbek Leaders Attend Muted Victory Day Parade in Moscow | The Diplomat
item.description: In their bilateral meetings, Putin emphasized how grateful he was that the Kazakh and Uzbek leaders came to stand with Russia.

item.title: 🌏 Why Is China Watching India-Vietnam Relations Carefully | The Diplomat
item.description: China appears to be closely monitoring the development of India-Vietnam relations, particularly in the areas of defense, maritime affairs, and strategic technology.

item.title: 📍 Dubious Delistings: Unexplained U.S. Sanctions Removals for the Corrupt and Well-Connected | Just Security
item.description: U.S. targeted sanctions have long promoted accountability, but recent removals under Trump raise concerns about opacity, favoritism, and weakening human rights enforcement. The post Dubious Delistings: Unexplained U.S. Sanctions Removals for the Corrupt and Well-Connected…
